channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Collingwood Magpies surge ahead in thrilling AFL clash against Adelaide Crows
item.description: In a high-octane AFL match at Adelaide Oval, Collingwood Magpies have taken the lead after a dramatic second-half comeback. The game, which saw intense rivalry between the Pies and Adelaide Crows, fea...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua Vows to Challenge Ruto's Leadership in Mt Kenya
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has vowed to mobilize Mt Kenya against President William Ruto's UDA party ahead of the 2027 General Election. Speaking at a requiem mass for two youth killed d...

item.title: Students Protest NEET Exam Irregularities in Patna, Police Use Water Cannons
item.description: Students in Patna protested against alleged NEET exam irregularities, leading to a chaotic yet viral demonstration. Organized by the All India Students' Association (AISA), the protest turned into a s...

item.title: NFL Players Advocate for Grass Fields Following FIFA World Cup Requirements
item.description: Saquon Barkley and NFL players are pushing for the league to transition to natural grass fields following the FIFA World Cup's requirement for grass surfaces. The NFLPA has humorously referenced provi...

item.title: Yanga Faces Challenges as Key Players Miss Early Season Matches
item.description: Young Africans (Yanga) are set to face significant challenges as three key players—Clement Mzize, Dickson Job, and Pacome Zouzoua—will miss the start of the 2026/27 season due to injuries. The absence...

item.title: Katy Perry's 'Lifetimes' Concert Film to Screen in UK Cinemas Following Fundraising for Music Venues
item.description: Katy Perry's 'Lifetimes' concert film, filmed during her sold-out Paris tour in November 2025, is set to screen in 150 UK cinemas and globally across 70 countries from September 2, 2026. The film, cap...

item.title: Wildfires in British Columbia Prompt Evacuation Orders and Alerts in Squamish-Lillooet Region
item.description: Wildfires continue to threaten communities across British Columbia, with new evacuation orders and alerts issued in the Squamish-Lillooet region. The Squamish-Lillooet Regional District has warned res...

item.title: A Nostalgic Look at South Africa's Beloved TV Sitcoms
item.description: This article celebrates South Africa's iconic sitcoms that shaped the nation's cultural identity. From the 1980s to the 2010s, shows like 'Sgudi 'Snaysi' and 'Going Up' captured the essence of townshi...

item.title: SONA 2026: Priority Legislation Doesn't Guarantee Law Passage
item.description: The article examines how presidents' priorities mentioned during State of the Nation Addresses (SONAs) often fail to become law, despite being labeled as 'priority legislation.' It highlights that ove...

item.title: Ontario arrests 63 in major internet child exploitation operation
item.description: Law enforcement in Ontario has made significant progress in combating online child exploitation, with 63 individuals arrested as part of a joint operation involving the Ontario Provincial Police (OPP)...

item.title: Macron's Courteous Gesture at Gabon's State Reception
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron and his wife welcomed Gabon's President Ali Bongo Ondimba and his wife, Marie Chantal Bongo, at a formal state reception at the Élysée Palace in Paris. The event, captured on...

item.title: Mississippi Congressman Calls for FBI Involvement in Mysterious Teen's Death
item.description: Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-MS), Mississippi's sole Black U.S. congressman, has called for federal intervention in the investigation of 18-year-old Nolan Wells' death after an independent autopsy failed t...

item.title: Experts Doubt Distillation Alone Explained Kimi K3's Success, Despite U.S. Allegations
item.description: The article discusses allegations by U.S. officials that Moonshot, the Chinese company behind Kimi K3, may have copied Anthropic’s Fable LLM to develop its model. White House science advisor Michael K...

item.title: Kenya's Mt Kenya by-election results signal political shift ahead of 2027 polls
item.description: Sammy Kamau, a young candidate from the Democracy for Citizens Party (DCP), won the Ol Kalou MP seat in Kenya's Mt Kenya region with a landslide victory, defeating the ruling United Democratic Allianc...

item.title: FG Allocates N500m for Strategic Communications Amid Genocide Allegations
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria spent N500 million in 2025 on an emergency strategic communications campaign to counter allegations of coordinated Western propaganda accusing the country of persecut...

item.title: Final Year Brethren Celebrations: Balancing Tradition and Financial Strain in Nigerian Universities
item.description: The article explores the evolution of 'Final Year Brethren' (FYB) celebrations in Nigerian universities, highlighting how these events have transformed from simple sign-out ceremonies into elaborate, ...

item.title: Chad Hugo Collaborates with Tierra Whack, Leikeli47, and Rochelle Jordan on New Single ‘JUMPUPW!NYA’
item.description: Chad Hugo, the former Neptunes and N.E.R.D. musician, has released his debut solo single ‘JUMPUPW!NYA’, a high-energy track featuring collaborations with Tierra Whack, Leikeli47, and Rochelle Jordan. ...

item.title: UK airport drop-off charges rise by average of £1.70, sparking debate over pricing and alternatives
item.description: Analysis reveals that drop-off fees at UK's major airports have increased by an average of £1.70 since last year, with some locations charging up to £10 for short periods. Gatwick Airport imposes a £1...

item.title: Analyst Ratings and Price Targets for Key Companies
item.description: The article provides an overview of analyst actions and stock performance for several Canadian companies. Rogers Communications Inc. (RCI-B-T) reported a second-quarter result that met expectations, w...

item.title: Tanzania Insight News Roundup: Key Updates and Stories
item.description: The article titled 'To kneel or not to kneel?' published on July 23, 2026, serves as a compilation of various news updates from Tanzania. It highlights several important developments across different ...
